event_info: to deep square leg; starts with a leg-side length ball; clipped past square leg and RR are away
that's a gift of a length ball on the pads from Siraj. Vaibhav didn't have to do much with no fine leg in place; just glanced it fine and he is away with a first ball four
short of length delivery on the stumps; Vaibhav looks to flick; misses; gets hit on the pad. Pitched outside leg for LBW

second gift of the over. Short of a length down the leg-side. The coach won't be happy with that line from his senior pro when there is no protection at fine leg. Vaibhav gets inside the line and pulls this really fine and collects his second boundary
full and bit of shape back in; Vaibhav drives to long-on
short of length delivery just outside off stump; Jaiswal looks to cut; cut off with a slide at cover-point
short of length delivery outside off stump; Vaibhav dabs to third man
short of length on the pads; tucked through backward square leg. Over to you; Vaibhav says Jaiswal
short of length just outside off stump; Vaibhav defends off the back foot towards cover-point
just as you thought they were bowling hard lengths; Kagiso Rabada looks for swing and bowls full down the leg-side. Very lucky that Vaibhav didn't tickle this fine
full and on the stumps; Vaibhav mistimes the drive to mid-on

uppish and safe! Didn't time this loft really well as the bat turned in his hands. Not quite full not quite short so Vaibhav couldn't get under the ball properly. Luckily for him he managed to clear mid-off and the ball ran away to the fence
short of length delivery; punchy back foot defence to the off-side

every time they have overpitched it's been a literal free-hit for these batters. Siraj will be kicking himself for offering a slot ball with some width outside off stump; Jaiswal says thank you very much and lofts over cover for a maximum

that is the third boundary Siraj has conceded by spraying the ball down the leg-side. This time it's short of length and down the leg-side. There is short fine leg this time but it still is a freebie and Jaiswal took toll
gets away with another spray past the leg-side. Fine nudge would have been another four
the short of length delivery on the stumps; Jaiswal defends

mistimed but it is still good enough to clear the infield. Not a bad ball. Short of a length just outside off stump; Jaiswal went for the loft through the line over mid-off. Backed himself to clear the infield; bat turned in his hands on impact but he got away with it

will go down as a chance but a toughie for Buttler! Short of a length delivery on the leg stump; Jaiswal got inside the line to whip it away fine past short fine leg; ball took the under edge and flew low to the right. Buttler did dive full length and get his right to it but it didn't stick. Could have been taken
blocked from the crease
yorker just outside off stump; Vaibhav digs it out to mid-wicket
nice steepler but too much height for me says the square leg ump and KG has a polite enquiry about it

lovely! The way he delayed the shot by opening the bat face to get it over point was commendable. Short of a length delivery outside off stump; Vaibhav wanted to get under it. Was pace off from KG; so Vaibhav had to wait that extra bit and he managed to slice it over the infield for four
direct-hit from Rashid could have been curtains. Vaibhav just took off after driving this length delivery straight to mid-off. Rashid took aim at the bowler's end but missed
148kph from KG and Jaiswal is beaten on the dab. He wanted to steer this short of length delivery to third man but got beaten for pace
now pace off; Jaiswal waits and defends

that's handsome! The shot of the game so far. 145.5kph short delivery and Jaiswal didn't care one bit. Just rocked back; swivelled on the pull and turned his back on the ball after making contact in the followthrough because he knew that was easily clearing mid-wicket

dropped but this is even tougher than the previous one. 147.4kph short of length delivery; Vaibhav threw everything at it so the outside edge was thick and went towards first slip. Buttler dived full length and got his left hand to it; parried it wide of short third man. Oh dear; Siraj has rolled over the ball trying to stop the boundary with the slide. Looks like he's hurt his groin area. Physio takes him on the sidelines for some treatment

audacious. This is a express bowler so he was hanging back to upper cut it and give the ball direction over backward point. The timing was so good and this was delivered around 149kph so it took the ball all the way
149.6kph short of length delivery; Vaibhav taps it to mid-off
nasty bouncer that forces Jaiswal to sway out to avoid getting hit by the ball. Belated attempt to ramp the ball but missed the ball
drags this wide of mid-wicket and he gets two
short of length delivery; Yashasvi pushes it towards cover
fullish delivery outside off stump; Vaibhav drives to cover and gets off strike
short of length delivery outside off stump; Jaiswal pushes it towards cover
smashed! On the rise and muscular over long-on. 143.2kph short of length delivery; Jaiswal lets the ball come; swung through the line and absolutely nailed it over the ropes

the side on angle shows a lovely image of how soft Jaiswal held his hands on the handle to just let this short of length delivery come; glance the bat and go behind square in the big gap. Got this boundary because of great field awareness and smart technique
driven down to long-on
good length delivery angling in; Vaibhav defends off the back foot
turning back in; Jaiswal slices this to deep cover

ball_event: out Caught by Glenn Phillips!! The stand-in skipper does it so well. Shortish delivery on the stumps
event_info: Vaibhav rocks back to pull; is rushed on the shot so doesn't time it well. GP settles under it at deep mid-wicket and takes an easy catch. Not his usual innings but still did the job for his team and drags himself off the field disappointed. Vaibhav Sooryavanshi c Glenn Phillips b Rashid Khan 31(18) [4s-5 6s-1]
fullish delivery; Jurel drives to long-on
knocked to square leg
flatter delivery; Jurel works it to square leg
absolutely no room; flicked to mid-wicket
full and angling in; Jaiswal drives to mid-off. Prasidh from round the wicket gets a hand on it in his followthrough
short of length on the stumps; Jaiswal defends to the left of the bowler
gun yorker from Prasidh Krishna. Jaiswal just about managed to dig it out to the on-side and he gets off strike
make it 1 run off 4 balls so far. Short of a length delivery on the stumps; Jurel rocks back and flicks to mid-wicket
short of length delivery outside off stump; Jurel dabs to third man

that is a high quality shot! Prasidh will think he actually a nailed a yorker on the stumps but Jaiswal backed away to free his arms and he squeezed this past backward point with great power. Just 2 off the first 5 balls becomes 6 runs now in the over. Still a fine over though
flatter delivery on the stumps; Jurel rocks back and flicks through square leg
flatter delivery on the stumps; Jaiswal drives down the ground to long-on

that's sprayed down the leg-side and Jos B couldn't do anything about it. Full and down the leg-side; Jurel wanted to sweep. In the process Buttler got blinded and he had no chance. Rashid will be livid for releasing the pressure
wristed to deep mid-wicket
googly just outside off stump; Jaiswal drives through cover
lets this googly come to him and flicks to deep mid-wicket
shortish delivery on the leg stump; Jaiswal pulls to deep mid-wicket
full delivery on the stumps; Jurel drives and picks out mid-off
dismissive! Just took a step across the stumps and belted this 145.7kph length ball on the rise with a lofted drive over cover. Deflating shot for a bowler
short of length delivery on the stumps; Jurel defends to the on-side
very full for you miss; I hit; Jurel flicks to mid-wicket
slower delivery just outside off stump; Jurel mistimes the slog through backward square leg
back of a length ball angling in; Jaiswal misses the flick; ball rolls to the on-side off the thigh pad
shot! Fraction short and Jurel is quick to pounce; googly at the stumps and Jurel uses the depth of the crease; almost whip-pulls it through mid-wicket and the timing takes care of the rest
90.7ks wrong 'un on off; Jurel nudges it square on the leg-side and races back for the second

Gujarat Titans lose a review. The appeal was confident from Jos Buttler. Rashid joined in. The duo had a discussion and decided to challenge the onfield decision. Drifting down leg; Jurel sits down and misses the sweep. The ball brushed the pad on the way and that was the noise
leg-break outside off; Jurel attempts the reverse sweep and is nowhere close to connecting
quicker and shorter; Jurel is rooted to the crease and is rushed into bringing his bat down; stabs it to mid-wicket
edged and four. This was tad short but the line was close to off; Jurel didn't mind that; he goes hard on the cut and gets a thick outside-edge; the ball races away fine to the third man fence
134.1ks angled across the left-hander; Jaiswal attempts the slash and the ball sneaks under his bat; does not carry through to Buttler as well

fifty for Jaiswal. It has been a composed innings. He has not gone hammer and tongs as he usually does. Has bided his time and now it is go-time. He walks the wicket; stays leg-side of the ball and creams the drive on the rise through extra cover. Mid-off was inside the ring and he didn't bother to chase it

1 run; premeditates the scoop - shuffles a long way across and bends low to play the shot; but Siraj goes full and Jaiswal gets an under-edge into the off-side. He's down on his back but Jurel calls him across for a quick single. The umpire though has given it as leg-bye
full pitcher at the stumps; Jurel flicks and picks out mid-wicket. Not the first time Jurel has missed out on that shot. He found the fielder against Ashok as well
streaky but Jurel and RR won't be complaining. Siraj goes short and into the wicket; pace off delivery and Jurel is a bit early into the pull; gets a top-edge and the ball flies away wide of Buttler

those last two balls turn it into an expensive over. Siraj goes for the yorker and misses it completely - dishes a full toss on the pads; easy pickings for Jurel as he deposits it into the deep backward square stand. He used the bottom hand and whipped it all the way into the crowd

in the air and safe. 141.6ks back of a length delivery; Jaiswal is backing away and closes the bat face to tuck it into the leg-side; gets a leading edge and the ball lands in front of the incoming mid-off fielder

nicely done! He missed out in the previous over but not this time. This is a slower ball bowled into the wicket; Jaiswal shuffles a long way across and laps it fine - to the right of Buttler and he can only watch it fly past him

ball_event: out Bowled!! Cleaned up! A fine knock comes to an end. Rabada was brought on exactly for this - get rid of the set batter. But his job was made easy by Jaiswal. He moved away to the leg-side
event_info: exposed his stumps and gave Rabada full sight; the good length ball is aimed for the stumps and it ends up flattening the off-stump. 145.6kph delivery and Jaiswal was beaten for pace. Yashasvi Jaiswal b Kagiso Rabada 55(36) [4s-6 6s-3]

whoa! Whippage first ball. Rabada goes too straight - on middle and leg; the length is full as well; Parag uses his bottom hand and whips it towards deep square leg. He gets enough to clear the fielder on the rope
145.7ks on a length and around off; Parag guides it wide of third man and scampers back for the second
short of length around the hips; Parag tucks it with soft hands in front of mid-wicket
just short of long-on. Did Siraj play it safe? Fullish delivery at the stumps; Jurel does not quite time the flick shot; hits it uppishly and Siraj collects it on the bounce

ball_event: out Caught by Kumar Kushagra!! Soft dismissal! Prasidh comes back and strikes
event_info: just like Rabada. Rajasthan Royals have lost wickets in consecutive overs. 142.3ks good length ball outside off; Parag is half-hearted with his push and the extra bounce that Prasidh generates; hits the higher part of the bat and flies to backward point. Kushagra went reverse cup and almost messed up; luckily the ball got caught in his right hand even as the left hand came off. Riyan Parag c Kumar Kushagra b Prasidh Krishna 8(4) [6s-1]
fullish delivery angled in; Hetmyer eases it to mid-off

appeal for a strangle down leg. Buttler and Prasidh were interested. Rashid has a chat with the duo and decides against the review. Short and angling down; Hetmyer swivels around and goes for the pull. Looks like there was a deflection off the glove. Was that a missed opportunity? We need UltraEdge for confirmation. Oh yes; there is a spike and GT will be livid
low full toss around off; Hetmyer pushes it straight to cover

Hetmyer too is off the mark with a six. Prasidh goes for the short ball; but he's bowling from round the wicket; so the angle is taking it down; Hetmyer takes it on and nails the pull into the long leg fence

back to back sixes. That missed opportunity is already costing them. Hetmyer should have been back in the dugout for a duck; but instead he's smoked two sixes. Another short ball and Hetmyer is not backing away; he swivels around and hammers the pull backward of square
googly down leg; Jurel misses the glance and umpire signals a wide

fabulous shot! Jurel uses his feet to Rashid - a rare sight. There was a bit of flight; that allowed Jurel to come down the pitch; gets to the ball and lofts it beautifully over wide long-on. It is time for him to step on the gas..
flatter and at the stumps; Jurel works it square on the leg-side and settles with a single
leg-lined delivery; Hetmyer nurdles it round the corner - backward square leg
quicker and skidding on; Jurel stabs it down the pitch and the ball bounces over Rashid
93.7ks tossed up close to off; Hetmyer pushes it past Jurel and calls for a tight single. Rashid chases; dives and tries to stop but not enough to prevent the duo crossing over
short and outside off; Jurel cuts square of the wicket and retains strike
144.7ks back of a length around off; Jurel makes room and guides it to third man
ufff! What wrists from Hetmyer. 145.7ks good length ball angled across. It seemed like Hetmyer got a thick inside-edge as he attempted the flick. But he used his wrists to whip it over mid-wicket

ball_event: out Caught by Mohammed Siraj!! Excellent catch from Siraj. That ball was travelling and Siraj does superbly to hang on. Ashok Sharma is pumped. The missed review has cost them 18 runs. Full pitcher around off
event_info: right in the slot for Hetmyer; and he goes flat and towards long-on. Siraj moves to his left and hangs while falling to the ground. Much needed wicket for the Titans because Hetmyer was looking in a dangerous mood. Shimron Hetmyer c Mohammed Siraj b Ashok Sharma 18(8) [4s-1 6s-2]
150.7ks very full delivery; close to yorker length; Ferreira jams it back to Ashok
146.8ks full and around the blockhole; Ferreira digs it back past Ashok and Siraj is inside the ring; he prevents the single

154.2ks - fastest ball this season. Right in the yorker zone; Jurel jams it out near his feet and has no clue where the ball is; Ferreira informs him and he quickly gets back into the crease as Ashok runs in to collect the ball

ball_event: out Caught by Glenn Phillips!! Rabada gets his follow South African. Rajasthan Royals just losing their way a bit. It is the hard length from Rabada - pounded into the three quarter area
event_info: gets it to climb on the batter; Ferreira heaves to the leg-side and gets it high on the bat; the ball skies towards deep mid-wicket. Phillips settles just inside the rope and takes it calmly. Donovan Ferreira c Glenn Phillips b Kagiso Rabada 1(3)
angled in from round the wicket; Jadeja pushes it off the inner half to mid-wicket

dropped! Siraj takes the catch but is backtracking; throws the ball up but not within the boundary rope; so the ball lands beyond. Fullish delivery; Jurel does not quite get under it and hence the miscue to long-on. Siraj takes it over his head and the power takes him back; he can do the rest perfectly and concedes a maximum
slower ball bouncer; too short and too high; Jurel can't reach it and it is signalled a wide for height

fifty for Jurel. This is an excellent knock. He took his time to get going and is now motoring along. He raises his bat and soaks in the applause of the dugout. Full delivery angled in; Jurel flicks it to deep mid-wicket and races back for the second
clipped to deep mid-wicket
off-pace delivery outside off; Jadeja throws his bat and is early into the shot; misses and the ball goes on the bounce to Buttler. Rabada is done with his spell and finishes with figures of 4-0-42-2

phew! That is some shot from Jurel. Not really a bad - back of a length around off; but Jurel brings the bottom hand into play and whips the heave into the leg-side. The pace of Ashok takes the ball over the rope
slower ball that is fraction short; Jurel waits and cracks it square of the wicket
144.3ks short and outside off; Jadeja reaches and cuts it square of the wicket; just a single
110.4ks slower ball outside off; Jurel lets it pass and collects the wide
148.9ks back of a length delivery; Jurel tucks it into the vacant mid-wicket pocket and races back for the second
104.4ks off-pace delivery; Jurel dabs it to short third and Jadeja quickly crosses over
loses his radar on the slower delivery again; slips it down leg and Jadeja is happy to leave
141.9ks short and outside off; Jadeja slashes and the ball climbs over his top-edge. Ashok's figures read - 4-0-37-1
full delivery on off; Jurel flicks it in front of square and the deep mid-wicket fielder had some ground to cover; easy couple
140.2ks full toss around off; Jurel mistimes the heave to deep mid-wicket
low full toss angled in; Jadeja makes room and flicks it to deep square leg
smoked! Prasidh misses his length - right in the slot and no half measures from Jurel; clean swing of the bat and gets plenty of height; also enough to clear the cow corner fence
140.7ks yorker close to off; Jurel digs it to cover and quickly crosses over
full and angling in; follows a backing away Jadeja and does not offer him room; the heave is missed and the ball rolls back down the pitch after hitting the pad
shot! Siraj misses the yorker by a couple of inches and that is enough for Jurel to put it away; uses his strong bottom hand and whips it in front of square; splitting long-on and deep mid-wicket
slower bouncer outside off; Jurel attempts the ramp but does not make contact

ball_event: out Caught by Jos Buttler!! Soft dismissal! No appeal from Siraj or Buttler. But Jurel starts walking. They all knew he had hit that. Another slower ball bouncer
event_info: Jurel is almost off the ground while attempting the ramp; gets a feather and Buttler takes a simple catch over his head. A superb knock comes to an end. Dhruv Jurel c Jos Buttler b Mohammed Siraj 75(42) [4s-5 6s-5]
excellent from Siraj. Just 4 runs so far. Can he finish it well? He nails the yorker and Archer can only dig it back down the pitch
138.2ks low full toss on off; Archer squeezes an inside-edge backward of square leg

Jadeja finishes with a boundary. Still a good over from Siraj - just 9 runs. He misses the yorker and dishes out a juicy full toss; Jadeja swings hard and though he didn't middle it; the placement was in front of square and splits the two fielders in the deep
that will help! Full and swinging down leg. Would have been a fine ball to a RHB and the keeper had no chance. Freebies to start the innings
short of length delivery; defended

in the airrrr; just over the leaping Tushar Deshpande. Short ball angling away outside off stump; Sai guides it to third man. For a moment he had his heart in the mouth as the fielder was in business. Luckily Archer's extra pace too it over the ropes
blocked
fullish and outside off stump; Sai drives and finds the man at mid-off
short of length delivery angling away; Sai looks to cut late but the ball escapes the outside edge
crunchy cover drive! Full and outside off stump; Sai leans forward and plays a good cover drive. Man at short cover dived to the right but couldn't stop the ball
short of length delivery just outside off stump; KK taps it towards mid-off and gets his first runs for GT
that's a wide way outside off stump and DJ had to throw himself a long way to the right to collect that ball. Jaiswal gives him a pat on the head for that gather
edged and flies past slip! Short of a length delivery angling away; ball nips away off the pitch; Sai closed the bat face early to play slightly leg-side. Got away with the thick edge
full and outside off stump; Sai leans forward and drives to cover
driven off the outside half of the bat to third man
good length delivery on the stumps; KK lets this ball come to him and defend
watches the ball closely and then defends this length ball to the off-side
good length delivery on the stumps; Sai defends to the on-side
short of length delivery on the stumps; Sai knocks it to mid-on
shot! That will give him a lot of confidence. Full and outside off stump; KK leans forward and drives to the left of mid-off; who gave up pretty early
short of length delivery; KK defends off the back foot
short of length delivery outside off stump; KK dabs to third man
Sai gets under this short of length delivery and cuts it aerial over point. Timing so right and it raced past the infield and into the fence. Archer livid with himself
wristy but cut off at deep mid-wicket. Slower ball on the stumps; KK waits and flicks wide of mid-wicket to get two
neatly glanced! Fine leg up and he bowled down the leg-side. Thanks for that gift says KK and clips it behind square to collect an easy boundary
full and on the stumps; KK leans forward and drives to long-on
full delivery outside off stump; Sai pushes it towards cover
short of length delivery angling in; Sai waits and flicks to mid-wicket
full delivery on the stumps; Sai drives to long-on

straightaway goes for the wide yorker and you can't really blame this on the bowler. Sai dug this out into the pitch and got it to bounce. Burger reacted late and let this go right through his fingers. He seems to have hurt the fingertips with that. Grimaces in pain
beaten! Short of a length delivery angling away; Sai looks to slash and misses
oh didn't know where the ball went. Short of a length delivery on the leg stump; Sai missed the pull; ball hit the thigh pad and lobbed past the stumps. Sai thought he could have a run
absolutely nails this off-side yorker and beats Sai; who was late to bring the bat down for a squeeze
118.4kph slower ball almost knocks over Sai. Short of a length delivery; Sai looked to slog and played early; luckily the ball bounced over the stumps
low full toss and Sai should have placed it better. Whipped past square leg and they have a man at the fence
low full toss; sliced it hard. Man at cover-point gets a hand on it and slows it down. Hunted down in the deep

RR have already switched to death bowling. They are trying yorkers and this time Sandeep Sharma bowls a low full toss. Sai easily flicks through mid-wicket and gets four more. Fifty-run stand for GT despite Gill not being available
good length delivery on the stumps; Sai flicks to deep square leg
uppish and safe! They had a man pretty straight at long-on but KK managed to get it straight enough to beat the dive. Slower delivery into the wicket; KK steps out and lofts straight
short delivery on the stumps; KK tries to ramp it fine past the keeper but misses
yorker tailing down the leg-side. KK misses the glance; ball brushes the pad and rolls to the left of the keeper
deft touch! Flatter delivery outside off stump; Sai lets the ball come and guides it past DJ and gets an easy boundary
now he waits and tucks this flatter delivery through square leg for two
shortish delivery on the stumps; Sai pulls and sends it to wide long-on
short delivery on the stumps; Sai rocks back and nails the pull; picks out deep mid-wicket
uppish push to long-off as he looks to drive on the rise
to deep mid-wicket; clipped through square leg and these two just breezed through that over
Sai welcomes the skipper with a crunchy cover drive! Too full and right in the slot; waits and nails it hard through wide long-off and the fielder in the deep had no chance
flatter delivery; Sai looks to defend; gets an inside edge that rolls to the on-side
plays the sweep early and gets hit on the pad. Maybe an inside edge

full toss and belted over mid-wicket. Gill or no Gill; nothing deters Sai. You can't shake his focus. Never felt the pressure of a senior batter's absence and even took pressure of the youngster as well. Every time this guy plays here he literally betters his venue average. This is Sai's 7th T20 fifty in Ahmedabad and his 13th IPL fifty overall. Great applause from the crowd and his teammates
dragged to mid-wicket

ball_event: out Caught by Shimron Hetmyer!! That's great catching and boundary awareness from Hety. Was so cool. KK got under this flighted delivery and lofted it high towards long-on. Seemed like a six but Hety
event_info: despite being so close to the ropes; stayed calm. Took the catch; released the ball before going over the ropes; came back in to complete the catch again. KK gutted as he looks at the replay. Decent outing for him but he walks back disappointed. Kumar Kushagra c Shimron Hetmyer b Riyan Parag 18(14) [4s-3]
worked away to mid-wicket
turning away outside off stump; Buttler drives to long-off
short and outside off stump; Sai rocks back and cuts past point. Races away to the fence
short delivery on the stumps; Sai rocks back and pulls straight to long-on
slog sweep! Full and turning back in; Sai gets down on one knee and muscles it high and handsome over deep mid-wicket
flatter delivery outside off stump; Sai guides it to backward point
1 run; short of length delivery on the leg stump; Buttler looks to flick; misses and the ball rolls behind square off the thigh pad
shortish slower delivery wide of off stump; Sai mistimes the slap. Man at mid-off dives to the right and cuts it off

touch! Was just a touch! Caressed that ball with so much love wide of mid-off. Back and across slightly; let this length ball come and gave the full face of the bat to get it wide of the fielder to collect his first boundary
short of length delivery outside off stump; Buttler dabs to point
oh early into the pull shot! Short from Nandre Burger; seemed like it didn't come on as expected. Buttler played with one hand and dragged it behind square

cheeky! Short ball at the head but he doesn't bother about it. Moves out of the way and guides it straight over Dhruv Jurel to leave no chance for him and any fielder. 11-run over keeps GT well within touch of that asking rate
fraction short but hurries on; Buttler mistimes the pull to long-on
1 run; 91.1ks googly on the shorter side; Sudharsan pulls to deep mid-wicket. Bishnoi has overstepped. Free-hit coming up..
fires it wide outside off as Buttler comes down the track; he has to reach for the ball and chops it past Jurel
wrong 'un around off; Sudharsan hangs back and knocks it to cover

ball_event: out Caught by Tushar Deshpande!! Bishnoi strikes in his first over! He was held back but does not take long to pick his first wicket. It is a big one as the well-set Sudharsan walks back. Googly once again
event_info: short and spinning away; Sudharsan rocks back to play the pull but the away spin induces the top-edge; the ball goes straight up and Deshpande comes in from long leg; takes it with both hands. He suggests that he lost it for a while behind the lights; but still managed to hang on. An excellent knock comes to an end. Sai Sudharsan c Tushar Deshpande b Ravi Bishnoi 73(44) [4s-9 6s-3]
wrong 'un that is short; Phillips hangs back and punches to long-on

Buttler ends the over with a boundary. Bishnoi is not changing his length. This was a leg-break which was short and wide; Buttler didn't overhit the cut - waited for the ball to come and just directed it square on the off-side. No chance for the sweeper to get around and stop
139ks back of a length around off; Phillips knocks it wide of cover and gets off strike
this is Buttler at his best. He shuffles across his stumps; waits for the 139.2ks good length ball to angle in and laps it fine to the left of Jurel. The ball almost travels the distance..
136ks short and outside off; Buttler cuts behind square and the sweeper does well to keep it to a single
short and bowled into the wicket; Phillips goes on his toes and cracks it square of the wicket

stand and deliver. Length delivery with ample width on offer; Buttler maintains a solid base and extends his arms to tonk it over extra cover. Mid-off was inside the ring and Buttler just hit this through the line

Buttler is on the charge. He's showing his range. Short and banged into the wicket; sits up nicely outside off; Buttler transfers his weight back and nails the pull in front of square. No need to run for that..

ball_event: out Caught by Jofra Archer!! Second wicket for Bishnoi. "200 T20 wickets for him - youngest Indian to reach the mark
event_info: informs Deep Dasgupta on air. This was the googly and not quite short for Phillips to play the pull; he does not get under the shot and miscues it to long-on. Archer comes in and bends low to take it inches from the ground. That's a good catch. Rajasthan Royals keep striking at regular intervals. Glenn Phillips c Jofra Archer b Ravi Bishnoi 3(4)
Sundar is off the mark with a boundary. Bishnoi overcooks the flighted delivery; feeds it right in the slot and Sundar does not miss out; lofts it straight back over Bishnoi and picks up the four

ball_event: out Caught by Ravindra Jadeja!! Third wicket for Bishnoi. Four and out for Sundar. The move to hold Bishnoi back is paying off - he is causing a massive dent in Gujarat Titans' line-up. Googly around leg
event_info: Sundar thinks this is in the slot as well; bends low and whips it to deep mid-wicket; straight to Jadeja and he ain't dropping that. Washington Sundar c Ravindra Jadeja b Ravi Bishnoi 4(2) [4s-1]
quick googly around off; Tewatia angles it to backward point
short and outside off; Tewatia cuts it square of the wicket
drifting into leg; Buttler tucks it through the mid-wicket pocket and keeps strike

ball_event: out Caught by Sandeep Sharma!! Wickets tumbling now. Gujarat Titans have gone from 127/2 to 133/5. They have lost 3 wickets for 6 runs. The dangerous Buttler is gone and Rajasthan Royals have gained the upper hand in this contest. Burger comes from round the wicket
event_info: bangs it halfway down the wicket; rushes Buttler on the pull shot and the top-edge flies to fine leg; Sandeep bends down and takes it low to the ground. Buttler was looking in ominous form but he departs; leaving the Titans in a spot of bother. Jos Buttler c Sandeep Sharma b Nandre Burger 26(14) [4s-5]
back of a length angled in; Shahrukh tucks it square on the leg-side and opens his account
back of a length close to off; Tewatia plays it with soft hands to the off-side
dismissive! Short of length delivery; sits up nicely outside off and Shahrukh muscles the pull towards deep mid-wicket. He middled it and the ball sailed over the rope comfortably
fullish delivery around off; Shahrukh eases it to mid-off

neatly done! Shahrukh getting the home crowd going again. Another short ball from Burger; angling in and Shahrukh thought about the pull; but adjusted to ramp it over Jurel. He arched back and got the elevation to clear the keeper
edged and four! Googly and the ball spins away; Tewatia did not seem to pick it; closed the bat face looking to work it into the leg-side; the outside-edge flies through the vacant slip region
whoa! He nailed this off the middle. No prodding or nudging from Tewatia. He reverses his stance; sits down and nails the reverse sweep all the way over deep backward point

ball_event: out Caught by Dhruv Jurel!! Bishnoi has the last laugh. Fourth wicket for him. What a spell! He's turned this game around. Wrong 'un which is short and outside off
event_info: Tewatia goes for the cut and gets an outside-edge. Sharp catch behind the wicket by Jurel. Tewatia knew and started walking immediately. The boundaries are flowing but the wickets are also tumbling for the Gujarat Titans. Rahul Tewatia c Dhruv Jurel b Ravi Bishnoi 12(6) [4s-1 6s-1]

that's beaten everything - inside-edge; stumps and Jurel. Googly that spins in sharply; Rashid is rooted to the crease and has a tentative prod. The ball beats the inside-edge and flies over the stumps. Jurel could not get his gloves behind the ball. But the umpire has given it as runs..

ball_event: out Shahrukh Khan Run Out!! 2 runs completed. There you go.. Another one walks back to the dugout. Needless! Floated up outside off
event_info: overpitched and Rashid drives it in front of square. Long chase for the sweeper and Jadeja gets there; stops the ball and goes over the rope before coming back and sending in a bullet throw to the striker's end. Jurel does the rest and Shahrukh is well short of the crease. It seemed to be Rashid's call for the third and Shahrukh responded; it was risky and they are made to pay. Shahrukh Khan run out (Ravindra Jadeja/Dhruv Jurel) 11(4) [4s-1 6s-1]
googly around off; Rabada stays rooted to the crease and looks to defend; beaten on the outside-edge
on a length and outside off; Rashid drives to sweeper cover
yorker aimed for the stumps; Rabada gets his front leg out of the way and digs it to cover; quick single nabbed
high full toss below the waist; but the situation is such that it asks for Rashid to be cautious; he opens the bat face and guides it past the backward point fielder

well; well! Who said Rabada can't bat in this format? Back of a length delivery around off; Rabada makes room and gives himself the space to free his hands; lofts through the line and sends it sailing over long-off
they have got the 10 runs for the over. Another full toss from Archer; Rabada eases it to mid-off and crosses over
145ks good length ball angled in; Rashid attempts the rooted-whip to the leg-side; mistimes it to mid-wicket
googly around off; Rabada hangs back and works it to long-on
shot! Short and outside off; it was the leg-break and Rashid didn't try to overhit the cut; he opens the bat face and almost directs it behind square on the off-side. No chance for the sweeper..
defended to the off-side and Rabada calls through for a quick single. Looks like they will not take him on
wrong 'un that skids across; Rabada is rooted to the crease and has a poke; beaten on the outside-edge
quicker and rushing Rabada into bringing his bat down; he manages to do so and stabs it to point

another 10-run over. They are keeping up with the asking rate. This is a fabulous shot. With minimal risk they manage to score two fours. This is a flighted delivery; but overpitched and Rabada goes down the ground; nice clean swing of the bat and the ball almost carries the distance. Bishnoi has finished an impressive spell. His figures read - 4-0-41-4

lovely shot! This is not a bad delivery but Rashid has played it exceptionally well. Yorker around off; Rashid takes a step out and bends low; opens the bat face and squeezes it behind square on the off-side; bisecting backward point and short third
on a length and close to off; Rashid guides it through backward point

Rabada is playing a blinder. Gujarat Titans need just 19 now. Slower ball outside off; but plenty of width and in the slot; Rabada bends his back leg and smokes it straight down the ground. That landed right in front of the sightscreen
low full toss outside off; Rabada reaches and slices it to sweeper cover; goes on the bounce
low full toss on the pads; Rashid flicks it backward of square leg and has to race back for the second. Jadeja was the fielder and Rashid had to hurry. Luckily the throw was not close to the stumps
15-run over. Yorker on off-stump; Rashid takes a step down and digs it towards point
144.1ks length delivery; there to be put away and Rashid has a heave across the line; ekes out an inside-edge onto the pad and the ball rolls into the off-side. Over to Rabada..
short and angled in; cramps the batter for room and Rabada swivels to play the pull; misses and is struck in the mid-riff; the ball rolls back down the pitch
once again slants it in and does not offer space to Rabada; the pull is mistimed through mid-wicket

Rajasthan Royals lose a review. Excellent decision under pressure. Full and straight; Rashid is rooted to the crease and attempts the whip to the leg-side; luckily ekes out an inside-edge onto the back pad and the ball rolls to the leg-side. Archer did not spot the edge and was up immediately; but the umpire did and he got it spot
yorker length on off; Rabada clears his front leg and jams it back to Archer. This is turning into a brilliant over

1 run; Gujarat Titans lose a review now. This was just desperation from Rashid and Rabada. High full toss outside off; but well below the waist; Rabada throws his bat and does not connect. The ball lands in front of Jurel and he does not glove it cleanly. The pair cross over for a bye.. Rabada's waist is 1.13 meters and the full toss was 0.92 meters
low full toss outside off; Rabada tries his best to reach it but can't; wide signalled. 10 from 6..
very full delivery; the line is outside off and Rabada squeezes the drive to sweeper cover. 9 from 5
nails the yorker outside off; Rashid can only squeeze it out to third man. 8 from 4
great yorker from Tushar again. Rabada could only dig it out to the bowler; who got a hand on it
this is brilliant from Deshpande. He's nailed almost every yorker; this line is around off and Rashid moves across to play the scoop; misses and it is another dot. 7 from 2 now..

ball_event: out Caught by Jofra Archer!! Excellent catch under pressure from Archer. Deshpande is pumped. Look at him celebrate. Even Parag is pumped and doing a intense fist pump. This is a length delivery as Deshpande misses his yorker
event_info: in the slot to be put away and Rashid throws everything behind the big shot; slices it wide of sweeper cover. Archer runs to his right; slides and hangs on with both hands. Rashid Khan c Jofra Archer b Tushar Deshpande 24(16) [4s-3]

Rajasthan Royals win by 6 runs. Tushar Deshpande; take a bow! He runs off and does the this-is-my-territory kinda celebration. Credit to Parag as well for trusting him over a seasoned campaigner like Sandeep Sharma and an international bowler like Nandre Burger. Coming to the ball: Low full toss; aimed for the stumps and Ashok swings hard; gets an inside-edge onto the pad and the ball rolls down the pitch. No one bothers what happened after that as RR's players were busy running towards Deshpande
